summary: Marketing surfaces for CLIs, libraries, infrastructure tools, and developer-facing APIs. Audience is technical, scanning for proof of correctness and integration depth before clicking 'Read the docs'.

patterns.name: Hero with code panel
patterns.summary: Single-column copy on the left and a syntax-highlighted code block on the right. The code block is the dominant proof artifact, not a screenshot.

variants.summary: Right pane is a terminal frame with prompt + output, mono font, dark background.
Right pane is a code block with inline callouts pointing at a key line.
Right pane is a tab bar across 2–4 languages, same example translated.

anti_examples: Not a hero with a product screenshot mockup — that is hero/split-mockup-right.
Not an animated terminal demo — that is hero/animated-terminal.

patterns.name: Animated terminal hero
patterns.summary: Centered or right-side terminal that types out a single representative command. Used when install-ability is the headline value.

variants.summary: One install or run command, paused once typed.
Command then a 3–5 line representative output.

anti_examples: Static code panels are hero/split-code-right, not this pattern.

patterns.name: Monochrome logo strip
patterns.summary: A single horizontal row of 5–8 customer or community logos rendered in a flat monochrome treatment, sitting directly under the hero.

variants.summary: Slow horizontal marquee, paused on hover.

anti_examples: Color-saturated logo grids are intentionally excluded — they read as advertising rather than proof.

patterns.name: Three-pillar feature grid
patterns.summary: Three feature cards in a single row, each with a small icon, a short title, and a 1–2 sentence description. Anchored under a single value pillar heading.

variants.summary: Icon to the left of a title-description stack.

anti_examples: Four-up grids belong in feature-system/four-column-spec; the three-column pattern is taller and more declarative.

patterns.name: Deep-dive with code aside
patterns.summary: An extended explanation of a single feature, with a heading, 3–5 bullet points, and a code snippet anchored on the opposite side of the layout.

variants.summary: Snippet left, copy right (used to alternate rhythm).

patterns.name: Three quote cards
patterns.summary: Three customer quote cards in a single row. Used by developer-tool sites to surface adoption signals without committing a full case-study section.

variants.summary: Each card includes a circular portrait.
Each card uses a small company glyph in place of a portrait.

anti_examples: Single full-width testimonials are excluded from this pattern.

patterns.name: Integration stamp grid
patterns.summary: A 4–6 column grid of small framed icons representing third-party integrations. Each cell is a uniform pill or square; no copy beyond the name.

variants.summary: Square cells with rounded corners and a hairline border.
Pill-shaped cells with the icon inline with the name.

patterns.name: Performance metric strip
patterns.summary: A single row of 3–4 hero numbers with a short caption underneath each. Used for latency, throughput, or scale claims with explicit units.

variants.summary: Large value on top, caption below.
Value with a small comparative delta or baseline note.

anti_examples: Generic 'X% faster' claims without units are excluded; this pattern requires an explicit unit per metric.

patterns.name: Docs-first conversion band
patterns.summary: A single full-width section near the page bottom presenting two equally weighted CTAs: a primary 'Read the docs' link and a secondary 'Start free' or 'Install' action.

variants.summary: Centered heading with two side-by-side buttons.
Left-aligned heading and copy, buttons on the right edge.
